description: When the user explicitly asks to use their own API key, the build uses the stored credential even when n8n Connect covers the type — explicit user choice wins over the gateway preference.

conversation.text: Build me a webhook that takes some text in the body, summarises it in a couple of sentences using an LLM with my own OpenAI key, and returns the summary.

outcomeExpectations: A Webhook trigger accepts an incoming request whose body carries the text to summarise.
An OpenAI-family chat model (an OpenAI node or a LangChain lmChatOpenAi) generates a short summary of the incoming text.

The chat-model node's OpenAI credential references the stored credential named 'My OpenAI' by id, and does NOT carry `__aiGatewayManaged: true` — the build honoured the user's explicit instruction to use their own key.
The workflow returns the summary text back to the webhook caller.
